data.title: SEMrush Executives Sell Shares to Meet Tax Obligations
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Obishchenko VitaliiChief Operating OfficerSell$62,086Form 4
Levin EugeniePresidentSell$81,510Form 4

SEMrush Holdings' executives have recently sold shares of Class A Common Stock to meet tax withholding obligations related to the vesting of restricted stock units.

Vitalii Obishchenko, the Chief Operating Officer, sold 5,293 shares on January 7, 2025, at a weighted average price of $11.73 per share, totaling $62,086. The transactions were conducted at prices ranging from $11.51 to $11.83. Following this sale, Obishchenko directly owns 838,659 shares of SEMrush Holdings.

Eugenie Levin, the President of SEMrush Holdings, sold 6,943 shares on the same date, with a weighted average price of $11.74 per share, amounting to $81,510. The sales prices ranged from $11.52 to $11.83. After the transaction, Levin directly owns 580,549 shares.

data.title: U.S. stocks got clobbered Friday despite a blockbuster jobs report. What's going on?
data.description:
By Joseph Adinolfi

Rising bond yields are heaping pressure on equities as investors fear a resurgence of inflation

U.S. stocks tumbled on Friday after a blockbuster December jobs report.

At first blush, that reaction might seem surprising to some investors. The labor market is expanding at its fastest pace since March, with job gains seen across a broader swath of industries. The resilience of the U.S. economy and labor market has received much of the credit for powering the U.S. stock market higher over the past two years. What changed?

The blame seems to lie with rising Treasury yields. They have been gaining rapidly even as the Federal Reserve has sought to lower short-term interest rates since September. Apollo Global Management Chief Economist Torsten Slok recently characterized this pattern as extremely unusual.

The yield on the 10-year Treasury note BX:TMUBMUSD10Y, seen as the benchmark for the U.S. bond market, gained nearly 80 basis points during the fourth quarter, rising at the fastest pace since the third quarter of 2022. It has continued to climb in January. On Friday, it traded as high as 4.79%, a level it hadn't seen since late 2023.

A number of factors have contributed to the increase in yields. Some have blamed "bond vigilantes" - a phrase used to described investors who sell bonds to protest a government's profligate fiscal policies. Others have blamed fears that a strong economy could help rekindle inflation. It was this last factor that appeared to be in focus on Friday.

Adding to the downbeat picture is the fact that recently, rates have been rising in Europe and Japan. Seemingly, China is the only major economy where bond yields have remained anchored.

These fears have contributed to a dynamic known on Wall Street as "good news is bad news" - that is, good news for the economy and labor market is bad news for the stock market. The upshot is that investors want to see a strong economy - but not so strong that it causes inflation to pick back up, forcing the Federal Reserve to start raising interest rates again.

The S&P 500 SPX sank in December as yields pushed higher. Losses have continued in January and the index was down nearly 1% on the month as of Friday's close, with Friday's declines wiping out gains from the start of the year.

The Nasdaq Composite COMP and Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA also tallied their biggest declines since Dec. 18, the day when the Fed delivered its most recent interest-rate cut.

According to the latest data, U.S. GDP has continued to grow at an above-average pace. Despite this, pockets of weakness have persisted, exacerbating investors' fears that interest rates could remain higher for longer.

The housing market has struggled with mortgage rates north of 7%. Commercial real-estate borrowers have managed to term out debt - hoping that rates will fall, sparing the market from a full-blown meltdown. But it's unclear how long these companies can hold out; the longer rates stay elevated, the greater the risk.

Manufacturing activity, which is also extremely interest-rate sensitive, has continued to struggle, as have lower-income consumers.

According to the latest Federal Reserve data, delinquency rates on credit cards have climbed to the highest level in nearly 15 years. Shares of companies like Dollar Tree Inc. (DLTR) and others that cater to budget-conscious consumers have tumbled.

Investors are concerned that, if borrowing costs don't fall and inflation rebounds, even wealthier consumers could start to struggle, causing corporate profit margins to shrink.

"The market is worried about the potential for continued heightened interest rates impacting companies and consumers and the potential for growth to restoke inflation in the future," said Brent Schutte, chief investment officer at Northwestern Mutual Wealth Management Co., during an interview with MarketWatch.

"There's also the fear that, at some point in the future, the Fed might have to hike rates again, which has raised the possibility of slower economic growth in the future."

Futures traders pared back their expectations for how many interest-rate cuts the Fed is expected to deliver in 2025 after the jobs data. The latest figures from CME Group show that one cut between now and December is seen as the most likely scenario. The Fed's target range for its policy rate stands at 4.25% to 4.50% after a 25-basis-point cut in December.

Adding to investors' anxieties, a team of economists at Bank of America said that they didn't foresee the Fed cutting interest rates at all in 2025. While it isn't their base case, they now see a rate hike as more likely.

Inflation has eased sharply from its peak in the summer of 2022. But the pace of its decline has more or less ground to a halt over the past few months, leaving the Fed's preferred price gauge above the central bank's 2% target.

In response, investors have dialed up their expectations for how high inflation might remain over the next few years. This is reflected in markets via something known as the term premium.

Derived from a statistical model developed by three Fed economists, Tobias Adrian, Richard Crump, and Emanuel Moench, the term premium is intended to explain how much additional yield bond investors are demanding to compensate them for the risk that interest rates might change over the life of their investment.

As of the end of December, the 10-year term premium stood at just under 0.5%, its highest level since 2014, Federal Reserve data showed.

Inflation is a big part of what's driving the term premium higher. But concerns about unchecked federal spending are also a factor, said George Cipolloni, a portfolio manager at Penn Mutual Asset Management.

"People are focusing on the term premium now, and it's telling you that they want more yield to take that risk," Cipolloni told MarketWatch on Friday.

The impact of all of this has been amplified by the fact that stock-market valuations are high relative to history, Cipolloni said.

President-elect Donald Trump's policy agenda also has contributed to investor unease when it comes to the stock market, said Robert Conzo, chief executive officer at the Wealth Alliance. Trump's tariffs could help revive inflation, while his plans for more tax cuts could cause the federal budget deficit to expand, adding more upward pressure on bond yields.

"The tone is one of a lot of uncertainty," said Conzo, despite coming off two years of more than 20% gains for the stock market.

"It seems as if the general investor just doesn't believe that markets are good, or will be good in the future. There's just a very pessimistic tone," Conzo said in an interview Friday with MarketWatch.

While the stock-market rally has suffered a setback recently, the S&P 500 has still risen more than 60% since its bear-market low on Oct. 12, 2022, FactSet data showed.

There are still plenty of reasons for investors to remain bullish. Corporate earnings growth is expected to be strong this year, which could help justify stocks' lofty valuations. Although the bar for another year of double-digit gains is high, the fact remains that momentum remains on investors' side, Cipolloni said.

The degree to which stocks can climb moving forward will likely depend on whether bond yields continue to climb. Investors could face another challenge next week when December consumer-price index data, a popular inflation gauge, is released. But the fourth-quarter corporate earnings season is also kicking off.

According to FactSet's John Butters, S&P 500 companies are expected to report their strongest earnings growth in three years. That could be enough of a distraction from inflation and interest rates to send stocks higher once again, Cipolloni said.

Joy Wiltermuth contributed reporting

-Joseph Adinolfi

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: CLS Holdings USA, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CLS Holdings USA, Inc., a diversified cannabis company operating in the dispensary and production segments, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter ended November 30, 2024. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements amidst a challenging market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $4.16 million, decreased by 20% compared to the same period last year due to lower sales in both dispensary and production segments.
  • Gross Margin: $1.77 million, representing 43% of revenue, slightly improved from 42% in the prior year period.
  • Operating Income (Loss): $(0.32) million, improved from a loss of $(0.44) million in the prior year period, reflecting cost-cutting efforts.
  • Net Loss: $(0.09) million, significantly reduced from a net loss of $(1.30) million in the prior year period, aided by gains on debt settlements.
  • Net Loss Per Share - Diluted: $(0.00), compared to $(0.02) in the prior year period, reflecting the reduced net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company's revenue is divided into two main segments: the cannabis dispensary, which accounted for 65% of revenue, and cannabis production, which accounted for 35% during the three months ended November 30, 2024.
  • Sales Units: The number of customers served at the dispensary decreased to 57,152 from 68,586 compared to the same period last year, indicating a decline in foot traffic or customer engagement.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company achieved a slight improvement in gross margin, increasing to 43% from 42% in the previous year, reflecting better cost management despite a decrease in overall sales.
  • Cost Management: Selling, general, and administrative expenses decreased by approximately 10% due to cost-cutting efforts, highlighting the company's focus on operational efficiency.
  • Future Outlook: The company aims to grow through acquisitions in secondary and tertiary markets, targeting newly regulated states, and intends to establish a regional presence in the cannabis industry.

SEC Filing: CLS Holdings USA, Inc. [ CLSH ] - 10-Q - Jan. 10, 2025

data.title: Equities Retreat, Yields Surge as Markets Weigh Jobs Report
data.description:
US benchmark equity indexes fell Friday, while Treasury yields soared as traders parsed official data showing that the economy added more jobs than projected in December.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average and the Nasdaq Composite fell 1.6% each to 41,938.5 and 19,161.6, respectively, while the S&P 500 lost 1.5% to 5,827. Real estate and financials saw the biggest drops among sectors. Energy was the sole gainer.

US stock markets were closed Thursday to mourn the passing of former President Jimmy Carter.

For the week, the Nasdaq declined 2.3%, while the Dow and the S&P 500 shed 1.9% each.

In economic news, total US nonfarm payrolls rose by 256,000 last month, the Bureau of Labor Statistics reported. The consensus was for a 165,000 increase, according to a survey compiled by Bloomberg.

"This was a robust employment report that reinforces the resilience of the US labor market and expansion and will keep the (Federal Reserve) on hold in January as they try to bring inflation pressures down," BMO said.

Markets are widely expecting the central bank's Federal Open Market Committee to hold interest rates steady later this month, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

US consumer sentiment dropped this month as year-ahead inflation expectations hit the highest level since May, preliminary results from the University of Michigan's Surveys of Consumers showed. Consumers' five-year inflation outlook increased to 3.3% from 3%, marking the third time in the past four years that the metric has seen such a large one-month change, according to the report.

The US two-year yield jumped 12.4 basis point to 4.39% Friday, while the 10-year rate gained 8.4 basis points to 4.77%.

In company news, Constellation Brands shares sank 17%, the worst performer on the S&P 500, as the beer and wine company lowered its annual sales outlook after its fiscal third-quarter results missed Wall Street's expectations.

Walt Disney's ESPN, Fox , and Warner Bros. Discovery said they will not launch their planned Venu Sports streaming service. Warner Bros. shares declined 3.6%, while Disney lost 1%. Fox's class A and B shares dropped 1.6% each.

Constellation Energy agreed to acquire privately held energy company Calpine in a cash-and-stock deal for a net price of $26.6 billion, including debt. Constellation Energy's shares soared 25%, the best performer on the Nasdaq and the second-best on the S&P 500.

Walgreens Boots Alliance was the top gainer on the S&P 500, up nearly 28%, after the drugstore chain logged better-than-expected fiscal first-quarter results buoyed by sales growth across its divisions. The company reiterated its full-year earnings outlook amid a challenging US retail environment.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil jumped 3.6% to $76.61 a barrel Friday as the US Department of the Treasury announced further sanctions against Russia by targeting its oil production and exports. This could boost crude demand from certain producers in the Middle East, led by Saudi Arabia, Saxo Bank said in a report.

Gold increased 1% to $2,716.70 per troy ounce, while silver advanced 0.9% to $31.30 per ounce.

data.title: Heard on the Street Recap: Wake-Up Call — WSJ
data.description:
By Heard Editors

The bond market has been signaling for some time that investors may be too complacent. On Friday the message finally broke through to stocks.

The trigger was a very strong monthly jobs report. The U.S. economy added 256,000 jobs in December, well above the 155,000 that economists predicted. That, along with resilient inflation readings in recent months, greatly reduces the odds that the Federal Reserve will lower rates at either of its next two meetings late this month and in March.

Indeed, it is dawning on investors that there may not be much help at all coming from the Fed this year. The likelihood as implied by futures markets that its target rate range will be unchanged by December of 2025 now stands at 28.8%, compared with just 5.2% a month ago. The chance it will be just a quarter point lower by then stands at 40.8%, up from 18.6% over the same period.

This shift in expectations has been one of the forces driving bond yields steadily higher. The others may include fears over high budget deficits, and the possibility that new tariffs under Trump could further juice inflation. On Friday the yield on benchmark 10-year Treasury note jumped 0.092 percentage point to 4.772%. Both the 10-year and the 30-year yields now stand at their highest levels since November of 2023.

That was unwelcome news for stocks. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell nearly 700 points, or 1.6%. The S&P 500 dropped 1.5% and the Nasdaq declined 1.6%.

Among standout individual names, it was a tale of two Constellations: Constellation Energy, the owner of America's biggest nuclear power fleet, rallied 25% after confirming reports that it is buying the owner of the largest natural-gas-fired power fleet. But Constellation Brands, which sells the Modelo and Corona beer brands in the U.S., plummeted 17% after reporting weak sales growth, blaming economic headwinds for slowing beer sales. Things could get even worse for the company-and indeed the broader market-if Trump follows through on threats to levy a steep tariff on all imports from Mexico.

This analysis comes from the Journal's Heard on the Street team. Subscribe to their free daily afternoon newsletter here.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: GrafTech International Files 8K - Regulation FD >EAF
data.description:
GrafTech International Ltd. (EAF) filed a Form 8K - Regulation FD Disclosure - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on January 10, 2025.

On January 10, 2025, the Company issued a press release announcing its entry into the Cooperation Agreement. A copy of the press release is attached hereto as Exhibit 99.1 and is incorporated by reference to this Item 7.01 herein.

The information furnished in this Item 7.01, including Exhibit 99.1, shall not be deemed "filed" for purposes of Section 18 of the Exchange Act, or otherwise subject to the liabilities of that section, nor shall such information be deemed incorporated

by reference in any filing under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, regardless of any general incorporation language in such filing, except as shall be expressly set forth by specific reference in such filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/931148/000093114825000015/gti-20250110.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/931148/000093114825000015/0000931148-25-000015-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: GrafTech International Files 8K - Entry Into Definitive Agreement >EAF
data.description:
GrafTech International Ltd. (EAF) filed a Form 8K - Entry Into a Definitive Agreement - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on January 10, 2025.

On January 10, 2025 (the "Effective Date"), GrafTech International Ltd. (the "Company") entered into a Cooperation Agreement (the "Cooperation Agreement") with Mr. Nilesh Undavia, who beneficially owns approximately 6.7% of the outstanding common stock, par value $0.01, of the Company (the "Common Stock").

Pursuant to the Cooperation Agreement, the Company has agreed to, among other things, (i) appoint Sachin Shivaram to the Board of Directors of the Company (the "Board"), effective January 10, 2025, as a Class III director whose term will expire at the 2027 annual meeting of stockholders of the Company, (ii) work in good faith with Mr. Undavia to find a mutually agreeable independent candidate to nominate for election to the Board as a Class I director at the 2025 annual meeting of stockholders of the Company (the "New Candidate") and (iii) appoint each of Mr. Shivaram and the New Candidate to at least two committees of the Board.

The Cooperation Agreement includes customary standstill restrictions and other provisions that remain in place until January 31, 2027, or until May 31, 2028 (such period, the "Cooperation Period") if certain conditions set forth in the Cooperation Agreement are met. The Cooperation Agreement also provides that Mr. Undavia has agreed to vote his shares of Common Stock in accordance with the Board's recommendations with respect to any proposal presented at each annual or special meeting of stockholders of the Company held during the Cooperation Period. During the Cooperation Period, the Company and Mr. Undavia have agreed to certain mutual non-disparagement provisions.

The Cooperation Agreement is attached hereto as Exhibit 10.1 and is incorporated herein by reference. The description of the Cooperation Agreement herein is qualified in its entirety by reference to Exhibit 10.1.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/931148/000093114825000015/gti-20250110.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/931148/000093114825000015/0000931148-25-000015-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: GrafTech International Files 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing >EAF
data.description:
GrafTech International Ltd. (EAF) filed a Form 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on January 10, 2025.

Appointment of Mr. Shivaram

On January 10, 2025, the Board appointed Mr. Shivaram as a Class III director of the Board, effective January 10, 2025, with an initial term expiring at the 2027 annual meeting of stockholders of the Company. Mr. Shivaram was also appointed to the Audit Committee and Human Resources and Compensation Committee of the Board. The Board has determined that Mr. Shivaram is independent under the New York Stock Exchange listing standards. Mr. Shivaram will participate in the Company's standard director compensation program for non-employee directors on a pro-rata basis, which is described on pages 20-23 of the Company's Proxy Statement for its Annual Meeting of Stockholders held on May 9, 2024, which was filed with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC") on April 2, 2024. The Company intends to enter into an Indemnification Agreement with Mr. Shivaram. The form of Indemnification Agreement was previously filed with the SEC on March 26, 2018 as Exhibit 10.15 to the Company's Registration Statement on Form S-1/A (Registration No. 333-223791) and is incorporated herein by reference.

There are no related person transactions involving Mr. Shivaram or Mr. Flanagan that would require disclosure pursuant to Item 404(a) of Regulation S-K.

Reclassification of Current Directors in Contemplation of Obligations under Cooperation Agreement

In contemplation of the Company's obligation under the Cooperation Agreement to nominate a New Candidate as a Class I Director, on January 10, 2025, Timothy K. Flanagan, a Class I director, submitted his resignation from the Board, effective immediately, and was immediately reappointed to the Board as a Class II director whose term will expire at the 2026 annual meeting of stockholders of the Company.

The information set forth in Item 1.01 of this Current Report on Form 8-K is incorporated into this Item 5.02 by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/931148/000093114825000015/gti-20250110.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/931148/000093114825000015/0000931148-25-000015-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1338 ET - Match Group is early in its multi-year turnaround effort and is likely to see a decline in revenue for the year, says JPMorgan's Cory Carpenter and Daniel Pfeiffer in a research note. Turnaround efforts for dating app Tinder have proven more difficult than expected and is expected to get worse in 2025 amid a tougher environment for the online dating industry. "We believe Tinder use and revenue trends need to stabilize for Match shares to re-rate higher," say the analysts, noting this may take a few years based on the company's own guidance. In 2025, they're watching a few key items including whether activist investors grow more vocal with CEO Bernard Kim's board seat up for renewal in May and product launches for Tinder. Shares are off 2.6% to $31.04. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0626 ET - UBS sees slowing service revenues and equity free cash flow consensus cuts at Tele2. The bank understands the midterm appeal, but says peers offer better momentum and value. Longer term, potential attractions include market exposure, dividend policy, capital expenditure normalization and potential consolidation in Sweden, among other things. In the near-term, however, UBS thinks Tele2 has weakening momentum, and looks unlikely to cover its dividend. "We believe the next few quarters will likely be negative for Tele2." A derating and consensus downgrades are likely before the mid-term story becomes more convincing, it says. UBS downgrades Tele2 to sell from neutral and lowers its price target to 102 Swedish kronor from 104 kronor. Shares fall 3% to 106.50 kronor. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0528 ET - Deutsche Telekom is well-placed for sustained growth in the U.S., UBS analysts write in a research note. "While German mobile competition is increasing, the bulk of DT's German revenue are fixed-line and investors may be more focused on the upside from U.S. domestic exposure post the outcome of the presidential elections," the analysts say. The U.S. market is healthy, facing less price competition than many European markets, they add. The U.S. bank raises its recommendation on the stock to buy from neutral and its target price to 33 euros from 31 euros. Shares are up 1.5% at 29.70 euros. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0032 ET - Tencent's overall business fundamentals and growth outlook remain intact despite recently being added to a Pentagon list with alleged Chinese military ties, OCBC analysts write in a note. However, the news may indicate rising geopolitical tensions and could be a share-price overhang until further clarity on the development, they say. Tencent's key businesses are value-added services and online advertising, which remain positive over the long term. The recent share-price selloff may be an overreaction and provides for a long-term accumulation opportunity, they say. OCBC maintains a buy rating on the stock at fair value of HK$560.00. Shares are last at HK$373.00. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

2319 ET - Xiaomi's core businesses likely remained resilient in 4Q of 2024, OCBC analysts write in a note. The company was a "star performer" last year as share prices more than doubled on year, they say. The strong share performance was driven by solid growth in its core businesses on resilient margins and stronger-than-expected EV business margins, they say. Xiaomi's smartphone segment gross profit margin may bottom out in 4Q with potential improvement ahead given higher average selling price and lower component cost, they add. OCBC maintains its buy rating for the stock with a fair value at HK$40.50. Shares last at HK$33.60.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

2219 ET - Thailand's telecom sector is likely to face some headwinds in 2025-2026, CGS International's Weerapat Wonk-Urai says in a research report as the brokerage downgrades the sector to neutral from overweight. Headwinds include slower organic growth and potentially higher regulatory risk owing to upcoming spectrum auction, the analyst says. Also, the sector's valuation of 3.4X estimated 2025 price-to-book-value ratio, which is two standard deviations above its six-year historical average, looks expensive to CGS. The brokerage maintains hold ratings and target prices of THB287.00 and THB12.70 for Advanced Info Service and True Corp., respectively. Advanced Info Service shares are 0.4% higher at THB277.00; True Corp. shares are 0.9% lower at THB10.80. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2020 ET - The U.S.'s planned restrictions on artificial intelligence chip exports are expected to have limited direct impact on Malaysian technology companies, given their minimal role in the global AI supply chain, RHB IB analyst Lee Meng Horng says in a note. While indirect effects are harder to gauge, the local tech sector's exposure remains low, he reckons. Lee remains optimistic about a stronger 2025, driven by a sector recovery supported by robust broad-based demand and replacement cycle. RHB maintains an overweight rating on the Malaysian tech sector and pegs Malaysian Pacific Industries, CTOS Digital, Coraza Integrated Technology and Datasonic Group as its top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1130 ET - Constellation Brands' marketing efforts put in place for its beer business in the second quarter to offset a softer macroeconomic backdrop that began affecting consumer spending during this summer bear fruit in the third quarter, CEO Bill Newlands says on a call with analysts. Still, beverage alcohol remains relatively unchanged in its share of total consumer expenditures, Newlands says. "We believe its lower rate of growth also reflects the adoption of broader value seeking behavior across consumer goods." The company isn't sure yet if the company will revert to more normalized spending behaviors in the current quarter, the executive says. Shares fall 13% to $189.74. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

0939 ET - Bitcoin stands to benefit from improved risk sentiment after Friday's stronger-than-expected U.S. nonfarm payrolls report, 21Shares crypto strategist Matt Mena says in a note. The data highlights the resilience of the labor market, alleviating fears of an imminent recession and boosting risk sentiment, he says. "This favorable economic backdrop serves as a significant tailwind for bitcoin, which tends to thrive in an environment of growing investor confidence." The data could provide the momentum needed for bitcoin to return above $100,000 again and surge past last year's record high. Bitcoin rises 2.4% to $94,347, according to LSEG. It reached a record high of $108,379 on Dec. 17. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0221 ET - BBVA's reduction of the acceptance level of its $12 billion-plus hostile bid to take over Banco de Sabadell is mostly a technical adjustment at the request of Spanish regulators, RBC Capital Markets' Benjamin Toms and Pablo de la Torre Cuevas say in a research note. BBVA said its offer requires more than half of the effective voting rights of Sabadell, excluding treasury shares. Regulators saw a need to clarify what BBVA's acceptance threshold was and the Spanish bank's move effectively means the minimum acceptance is reduced by 0.7%, according to RBC. "We continue to be of the view that this deal gets done, probably by [the first half], and probably with some relatively minor remedies and a 10% increase in the offer price," the analysts say. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

2138 ET - Current market expectations and trading multiples have set a very high bar for Australia's banks in 2025, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a note. "While multiples have remained elevated for many months, we think a de-rating is likely in the year ahead," they say. As share prices stand, meaningful upgrades to consensus EPS and dividend forecasts appear to already be priced in. "There is very little margin for error," say the analysts. They prefer nonbank financial stocks in Australia this year. Among major Australian banks, MS's order of preference is overweight-rated NAB, followed by equal-weight-rated ANZ, and then underweight-rated Westpac and CBA. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2020 ET - The U.S.'s planned restrictions on artificial intelligence chip exports are expected to have limited direct impact on Malaysian technology companies, given their minimal role in the global AI supply chain, RHB IB analyst Lee Meng Horng says in a note. While indirect effects are harder to gauge, the local tech sector's exposure remains low, he reckons. Lee remains optimistic about a stronger 2025, driven by a sector recovery supported by robust broad-based demand and replacement cycle. RHB maintains an overweight rating on the Malaysian tech sector and pegs Malaysian Pacific Industries, CTOS Digital, Coraza Integrated Technology and Datasonic Group as its top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1511 ET - Oil futures gain for a third straight week, reaching multi-month highs as the Biden administration tightened sanctions on Russian oil while winter weather lifts demand for heating fuels and threatens production freeze-offs. The reaction to the sanctions and the weather is "somewhat temporary," says Rebecca Babin of CIBC Private Wealth US. "I think rallies above $80 Brent are going to get sold eventually, because of the spare capacity that's out there, and we know that if we keep going higher OPEC is going to come back with production." WTI settles up 3.6% to $76.57 a barrel Brent settles 3.7% higher. The benchmarks hit $77.86 and $80.75 intraday, respectively. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1445 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil and gas in Canada rose by 122 this week to 216, recovering from some large declines in December, according to data from oil services company Baker Hughes. Oil rigs were up by 100 at 144 and gas rigs increased by 22 to 72. Winter is typically a strong drilling season in Canada because there is no need to compensate farmers for lost crops on the fields, and many companies use the winter to delineate reservoirs, says Rystad Energy's Susan Bell. The Christmas break could be behind the low count of previous weeks, she adds. Canadian rig counts also drop during the spring thaw when the ground becomes mucky and hard to move around and road bans limiting axle weight on trucks makes it tough to move rigs on roads, Bell adds. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1415 ET - Constellation Energy's acquisition of Calpine for $16.4 billion is set to create the nation's largest independent power producer, Gabelli Funds' Tim Winter says in a research note. He views the merger favorably, as it comes at an attractive price, should boost Constellation's earnings by $2 a share, and would expand and diversify Constellation's regional presence in the nonregulated markets of Texas, California and the PJM Interconnection, a regional transmission organization operating in 13 mid-Atlantic states and the District of Columbia. Constellation will now be bigger in Texas while having less overall exposure to the PJM region, Winter says. The supply-demand situation in those regions appears very favorable, he says. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1355 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. fell by two this week to 480, and was down by 19 from a year ago, oil services company Baker Hughes reports. The oil rig count has remained below 500 since May of 2024 with producers taking advantage of drilling and well efficiency to raise production to a record level. U.S. crude oil output has been around 13.6 million barrels a day for the past five weeks, according to EIA estimates. Rigs directed at natural gas decreased by three this week to 100, compared with 117 a year ago, Baker Hughes says. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1103 ET - Crude futures test new multi-month highs after a Reuters report that the U.S. plans to expand sanctions on Russian oil. "I think these reports are being taken more seriously now because we're starting to see China and India back away from actually buying the sanctioned barrels," says Rebecca Babin of CIBC Private Wealth US. Previous buyer reaction to increased sanctions was "yeah, whatever, you're not going to enforce, now they're taking it a bit more seriously," she adds. The other factor pushing oil higher is winter weather driving up heating oil demand. WTI is up 3.2% at $76.27 a barrel, and Brent is 3% higher at $79.23 after touching highs of $77.86 and $80.75, respectively. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0807 ET - The winter weather that has lifted natural gas prices is extending to oil as concerns about supply from Russia and Iran coincide with higher demand for heating fuels. "Weather roulette has hit the oil patch this morning," with American and European models showing extreme cold for Texas and raising fears of freeze-offs, TP ICAP's Scott Shelton says in a note. While ensemble runs aren't as cold, "the risks are clearly there for Texas with possibly some refinery freezing issues as well," he adds. WTI is up 3.5% at $76.48 a barrel, and Brent is 3.5% higher at $79.62 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0721 ET - The U.K. has less than a week of gas demand in store due to colder-than-usual weather in the country and the end of Russian gas pipeline supplies through Ukraine on December 31, 2024, Chris O'Shea, chief executive of Centrica, says. As of Thursday, U.K. storage sites were 26% lower than at the same time last year, leaving them around half full, the group says. O'Shea says the U.K. needs to boost its energy storage capacity as it becomes increasingly reliant on renewable sources. The U.K. has some of the lowest levels of gas storage in Europe at 12 days average or 7.5 peak winter days, compared to Germany at 89 days, and France at 103 days, the company notes. Centrica shares are up 0.45% at 1.33 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

2118 ET - The progress of Dialog Group's Baram Junior Cluster field development is progressing positively and could strengthen the company's domestic upstream portfolio, RHB IB analyst Sean Lim says in a note. The project recently received a final investment decision, with an estimated capital expenditure of US$235 million. Following the approval, Dialog is set to begin a two-year development phase, Lim notes. The project is targeting first gas production by end-2026, and could contribute approximately MYR40 million annually to Dialog's earnings, assuming a project internal rate of return of 12%, he adds. RHB raises Dialog's target price to MYR3.11 from MYR3.09, while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 0.5% lower at MYR1.83. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1654 ET - An artificial intelligence-fueled boom of data-center construction is lifting demand for electricity and creating opportunities for developers of tools that can help make power systems more resilient to weather-related disruptions, says Jay Koh, a managing director at Lightsmith Group in New York. The private-equity firm last year invested in artificial-intelligence startup AiDash, whose software helps utilities keep trees away from power lines to reduce wildfire risks. "As you see increasing volatility due to extreme weather events, whether it's fire risk or storm risk or flood risk or heat waves, we think that there's going to be enormous amounts of demand for resilience of the energy system," Koh says. (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1107 ET - Gold futures jumped after the release of the jobs report--which showed 256,000 jobs being added in December, more than expected by the market. "Gold's strength following a robust jobs report might seem counterintuitive, as it typically suggests a stronger dollar and higher yields, which are headwinds for gold," says Alex Ebkarian of Allegiance Gold. "But this reaction highlights a broader unease among investors--it reflects deeper fears about structural issues like unsustainable debt levels, overvalued markets, and geopolitical uncertainty." Gold is now up 1.5%, to $2,732 an ounce--nearing the record of $2,800 an ounce set in October. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0917 ET - Deutsche Bank expects SSAB to report a weak set of fourth quarter numbers, driven by weak demand and further price and margin pressure across all core units. It forecasts below-consensus Ebitda of 1.2 billion Swedish kronor and cuts its estimates by 1%-3% for 2024-2025. "We expect U.S. plate prices to remain in a tough spot as demand remains weak," analyst Bastian Synagowitz says. Deutsche Bank lowers its price target to 55 kronor from 58 kronor and keeps its hold rating. Shares trade 0.5% lower at 43.56 kronor. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

2118 ET - The progress of Dialog Group's Baram Junior Cluster field development is progressing positively and could strengthen the company's domestic upstream portfolio, RHB IB analyst Sean Lim says in a note. The project recently received a final investment decision, with an estimated capital expenditure of US$235 million. Following the approval, Dialog is set to begin a two-year development phase, Lim notes. The project is targeting first gas production by end-2026, and could contribute approximately MYR40 million annually to Dialog's earnings, assuming a project internal rate of return of 12%, he adds. RHB raises Dialog's target price to MYR3.11 from MYR3.09, while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 0.5% lower at MYR1.83. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1957 ET - Posco Holdings could post below-consensus 4Q earnings on narrower profit margins, Daishin Securities analyst Lee Tae-hwan writes in a note. The South Korean steelmaker may have sequentially recovered in sales, which likely topped 8.4 million metric tons of steel in volume for the October-December period. However, its profit margins may have weakened due to lower steel-product prices, Lee says. He estimates Posco's 4Q operating profit at KRW474 billion, well below the market consensus forecast of KRW698 billion. Daishin cuts its target price for the stock by 13% to KRW410,000 and keeps a buy rating. Shares are 1.0% lower at KRW258,500. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1241 ET - Europe in February, why not? Winter is typically a slow time for transatlantic travel, but Delta President Glen Hauenstein says this year has been different. American tourists see their money go farther in Europe right now: "Europe is an incredibly screaming buy as a tourist destination," he says. And with smaller crowds and decent weather--particularly in southern Europe, travelers are finding winter trips have their appeal. Hauenstein says he doesn't believe the new appetite for winter travel will hamper the more popular summer season. "We're seeing robust demand in the off-peak, and I'm sure that the peak is going to be even better," he says. (alison.sider@wsj.com; @alyrose)

0752 ET - Renault stock is still rated as abuy, but the world is getting more complicated, Citi analysts Harald C Hendrikse and Soumava Banerjee write. Excluding Ferrari, Renault was the best performing stock in European autos in 2024. The French carmaker's management executed well and earnings were boosted by a disciplined sales channel strategy, new model roll-out with improved margins and a lack of China exposure. The company also benefited from much-improved free cash flow and balance sheet. However, risks from worsening macro and EU carbon dioxide regulations suggest the risk-reward has deteriorated, Citi says. Citi closes its short-term upside view and removes Renault from its focus list. It has a 52 euro target price on the stock. Shares trade 0.5% lower at 45.59 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0709 ET - Delta doesn't see travel demand slowing down in 2025. CEO Ed Bastian notes consumers are increasingly opting for the airline's premium products and experiences, a phenomenon happening across the industry that more airlines are leaning into and prioritizing. A snapshot of this demand came in November and December when Delta saw four of the top 10 revenue days in its history and double-digit growth in cash bookings driven by both leisure and corporate travelers. President Glen Hauenstein says revenue growth in 4Q came in ahead of its guidance as demand trends accelerated through the quarter and expects adjusted revenue in the March quarter to be up to 9% higher than 2024. Delta climbs 5.3% premarket. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0703 ET - Stellantis shares are cheap, but 2025 remains uncertain, Citi analysts Harald C Hendrikse and Soumava Banerjee write. Although the company isn't Citi's favorite pick, it understands why investors may look at it again. Stellantis was the worst performer in European autos last year, but the company's U.S. exposure versus no China exposure, continued strong U.S. market demand and weak sentiment could see the shares trade higher in early 2025 as investors hope for a recovery. "We still prefer other names on lower valuation (Renault, Volkswagen), and Porsche on earnings stabilization." It raises its target price to 13 euros from 12.40 euros, opens a 90-day positive short-term view, and keeps a neutral rating. Shares rise 1.4% to 12.59 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0647 ET - The U.K. Competition and Markets Authority's investigation into Global Business Travel's planned $570 million bid for CWT Holdings is fatally flawed, a filing from both companies says. A CMA probe found in November that the two companies compete closely for multinational customers and their merger risks reducing choice and raising prices. The companies hit back saying the CMA's definition of their market was "fundamentally erroneous". They said the regulator's interim decision was "fatally undermined by a biased and unrepresentative market investigation," and insisted they faced strong competition from other travel companies as well as hotels and airlines. GBT and CWT offered concessions, including price commitments and selling off some customer contracts to a rival, to get their deal greenlit by CMA's provisional Jan. 26 deadline and to end the probe. (edith.hancock@wsj.com)

0250 ET - ComfortDelGro faces increased competition in 2025, analysts at UOB Kay Hian write in a note. The Singapore-based company raises its taxi platform fees to offset a new industry-wide rule requiring higher contributions to platform workers' state-run pension plans. Meanwhile, two new competitors enter the ride-hailing market, further intensifying competition, the analysts note. A bus contract currently operated by ComfortDelGro's subsidiary, SBS Transit, is up for tender. This poses a near-term earnings risk due to competitive bidding, which is likely to result in lower margins, they add. UOB Kay Hian reduces its 2025-2026 profit after tax and minority interests estimates by 2%-3%. While maintaining a buy rating, the brokerate trims its target price to S$1.77 from S$1.83. Shares are flat at S$1.44. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: ARway.ai Issues Stock Options
data.description:
TORONTO, ON / ACCESSWIRE / January 10, 2025 / ARway.ai ("ARway" or the "Company") (OTCQB:ARWYF)(FSE:E65) is an AI powered Augmented Reality Experience platform with a disruptive no-code, no beacon spatial computing solution enabled by visual marker tracking with centimeter precision announces the issuance of stock options.

ARway.ai issued Peter Bloch, a successful entrepreneur and new BOD member 50,000 2-year stock options exercisable at .055/share. The company also issued 500,000 2-year .055/share options to Anum Wagas CFO. BOD member Anthony Pizzonia was also issued 50,000 2 year stock options at .055/share.

Bio:

Peter Bloch was the Chief Executive Officer of BresoTEC Inc. Mr. Bloch was also the CEO & Chairman of Bionik Laboratories from 2012 to 2018. Mr. Bloch is a CPA, CA, with a track record of building both public and private technology companies, mainly in the life sciences industry. In these roles, Mr. Bloch has secured significant funding for both private and public companies, gained experience with initial public offerings and led a number of acquisitions and partnership transactions. His past 25 years of executive management experience includes serving as Chief Financial Officer and joint interim CEO of Sanofi Canada Inc., the Canadian affiliate of Sanofi -Aventis, a global healthcare leader; Chief Financial Officer of Intellivax Inc., a biotechnology company which was sold to GlaxoSmithKline for $1.75 billion; founder of Tribute Pharmaceuticals, a specialty pharmaceutical company; the Chief Financial Officer of Gennum Corporation, a public semiconductor company focused on the TV and medical device market and Chief Financial Officer of Just Energy, a large public electricity and gas company . These companies have ranged in size from start-ups to companies with revenues of over $2 billion. Mr. Bloch also has substantial experience serving on the Board of Directors of both public and private companies. Mr. Bloch also serves as an advisor to Mars Discovery District, a large Canadian incubator for technology companies.

Sign up for Investor News - HERE

To learn more about ARway, please follow on Social Media: Twitter, YouTube, Instagram, LinkedIn, and Facebook, and visit our website: www.arway.ai

About ARway.ai

ARway.ai (OTCQB: ARWYF) (FSE: E65) is a spatial computing platform powered by artificial intelligence (AI) providing an array of augmented reality (AR) experiences for indoor spaces. ARway's breakthrough no-code no-beacon IPN allows for the easy creation of navigation, tours, information sharing, notifications, advertising and gamification. ARway works seamlessly as a cross platform solution on iOS/ Android. ARway's technology is optimized for both mobile devices and AR glasses: Apple's Vision Pro, Magic Leap and Microsoft's HoloLens. ARway has unlimited use cases for augmenting physical spaces, making it a valuable tool for creators, brands and companies in various industries. The complete ARway platform includes: the Web Creator Studio, the ARwayKit Software Development Kit (SDK) and a mobile app for iOs and Android.

Nextech 3D.ai

On October 26, 2022, ARway.ai. was spun-out from its parent Company, Nextech3D.ai (OTCQX: NEXCF) (FSE: 1SS). Nextech retained a control ownership in ARway.ai. with 13 million shares, or a 50% stake. Nextech3D.ai is a Generative AI powered 3D modeling Company and leading provider of augmented reality ("AR") experience technologies and 3D model services. Nextech's AI-powered 3D modeling platform, "ARitize3D" has contracts with; AMZN, KSS, CB2, Genuine Parts & many others. To learn more about Nextech3D.ai, visit www.nextechar.com

For further information, please contact:

Investor Relations Contact

investor.relations@arway.ai

ARway.ai

Evan Gappelberg

CEO and Director

866-ARITIZE (274-8493)

Forward-looking Statements 

The CSE has not reviewed and does not accept responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

data.title: S&P 500 Posts Weekly Drop as Stronger-Than-Expected Jobs Data Dim Rate-Cutting Hopes
data.description:
The Standard & Poor's 500 index fell 1.9% this week as data showing stronger-than-expected December job growth curtailed hopes for interest rate cuts this year.

The market benchmark ended Friday's session at 5,827.04. The weekly move came in four sessions as the US stock market was closed on Thursday for a national day of mourning honoring former President Jimmy Carter.

This marks the S&P 500's second consecutive weekly drop. It is now down 0.9% for the month and year.

Government data showed the US economy added more jobs than anticipated in December while the unemployment rate unexpectedly declined. Nonfarm payrolls rose by 256,000 last month, surpassing Bloomberg's consensus estimate for an increase of 165,000. The unemployment rate fell to 4.1% from November's 4.2%. Analysts expected the rate to stay at 4.2%.

The real estate sector was hit hardest this week, falling 4.1%, followed by a 3.1% decline in technology and a 2.7% drop in financials.

Among the real estate sector's decliners, shares of BXP and Federal Realty Investment Trust shed 9% and 7.3%, respectively.

In technology, shares of ON Semiconductor fell 16% amid negative analyst actions. The company received an investment rating downgrade from Truist and price target reductions from analysts at Deutsche Bank and Goldman Sachs.

Energy led gainers, rising 0.9%, followed by a 0.5% advance in health care and a 0.1% increase in materials.

The energy sector's climb coincided with a rise in crude oil futures. Gainers included shares of Texas Pacific Land , which rose 7.4% on the week, and Devon Energy , up 6.1%.

Investors next week will be focused on inflation data, with the December producer price index expected on Tuesday, followed by the December consumer price index on Wednesday. Later in the week, December retail sales are due on Thursday while December housing starts and building permits are set to be released on Friday, among other reports.

Also, the earnings reporting season will begin for the last three months of 2024. Results are expected from a number of financial companies including JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), BlackRock (BLK), Citigroup (C), Bank of America (BAC) and Morgan Stanley (MS). UnitedHealth Group (UNH) is also scheduled to release quarterly results.

data.title: Phreesia COO Evan Roberts Sells Shares Under Trading Plan
data.description:
Reporter NameRoberts Evan
RelationshipChief Operating Officer
TypeSell
Amount$74,940
SEC FilingForm 4

Phreesia's Chief Operating Officer, Evan Roberts, sold a total of 2,681 shares of common stock on January 8, 2025, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on July 17, 2024. The sales were conducted at weighted average prices of $27.6161 and $28.5247, with the total sale amounting to $74,940. Following these transactions, Roberts directly owns 769,993 shares of Phreesia's common stock.

SEC Filing: Phreesia, Inc. [ PHR ] - Form 4 - Jan. 10, 2025

data.title: Candel Therapeutics Executives Sell Shares to Cover Tax Obligations
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Nichols William GarrettChief Medical OfficerSell$88,749Form 4
Tyagarajan SeshuChief Technology OfficerSell$119,533Form 4
Tak Paul PeterChief Executive OfficerSell$173,995Form 4
Schoch CharlesOfficerSell$81,943Form 4
Barone FrancescaChief Scientific OfficerSell$112,367Form 4

Several executives at Candel Therapeutics have recently sold shares of the company's common stock to cover tax withholding obligations related to the vesting of restricted stock units, as per transactions reported on January 8, 2025.

William Garrett Nichols, the Chief Medical Officer, sold 10,799 shares at a price of $8.2183 each, totaling $88,749. Following this sale, Nichols directly owns 89,748 shares of the company.

Tyagarajan Seshu, Chief Technology Officer, disposed of 14,545 shares at $8.2182 per share, with the total amounting to $119,533. Post-transaction, Seshu's direct ownership stands at 111,112 shares.

Tak Paul Peter, the CEO, sold 21,172 shares at the same price of $8.2182 each, for a total of $173,995. This leaves Peter with a direct ownership of 273,616 shares.

Charles Schoch, the Interim Chief Financial Officer, sold 9,971 shares at $8.2182 each, totaling $81,943. Schoch now directly owns 52,549 shares.

Francesca Barone, Chief Scientific Officer, sold 13,673 shares at $8.2182 each, totaling $112,367. Following her transaction, Barone directly owns 124,207 shares.

data.title: Artisan Partners Asset Management Preliminary Assets Under Management Decrease in December
data.description:
Artisan Partners Asset Management late Friday reported preliminary assets under management of $161.21 billion at the end of December 2024, compared with $166.82 billion in November 2024.

Artisan Funds and Artisan Global Funds accounted for $77.6 billion of the total AUM in December, while separate accounts and other AUM totaled $83.6 billion, the company said.

data.title: Coca-Cola Consolidated Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $2.50 Per Share; Payable Feb. 7 to Shareholders of Record on Jan. 24
Nordstrom Raises Full-Year Outlook After Strong Holiday Season
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Nordstrom increased its full-year outlook, citing a stronger-than-expected holiday shopping season.

The department-store chain on Friday said it now expects revenue, including retail sales and credit card revenues, to grow 1.5% to 2.5% year-over-year. It had previously expected revenue growth to be flat to 1%.

Comparable sales, which adjust for store openings and closings, are now projected to rise 2.5% to 3.5% from last year, compared with a prior outlook of 1% to 2%.

These increases come after the company's sales in November and December exceeded expectations, Chief Executive Erik Nordstrom said.

For the nine-week holiday period ended Dec. 30, total sales were up 4.9% from last year's comparable frame. Same-store sales were up 5.8%.

Sales at the company's namesake banner rose 3.8%, or 6.5% on a same-store basis, while sales at its Nordstrom Rack banner jumped 7.4%, or 4.3% on a same-store basis.

Also during the holiday shopping season, the founding family of Nordstrom inked a $4 billion deal to take the retailer private.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com
